title: Async completion bridge — CQE to process wakeup
purpose: B-100 provides the CompletionRing trait that submits I/O operations and polls for completions. But completions arrive on the ring thread — they need to reach the BEAM process that initiated the I/O. This brief builds the bridge: when a process submits an I/O operation, it suspends (enters Waiting state). When the completion arrives, the bridge wakes the process with the result in its mailbox or x-registers, and the scheduler resumes it. This is the BEAM port model reimagined for completion I/O: instead of a port process mediating I/O, the process directly submits to the ring and suspends. The bridge uses a dedicated completion poller thread (from the dirty scheduler pool, B-077) that continuously polls the ring and dispatches results to the appropriate processes via the existing wake mechanism (wake_condvar, process status transitions).
task: Create an IoCompletionBridge that runs on a dirty scheduler thread, continuously polls the CompletionRing for completions, and dispatches results to waiting processes. Add an IoRequest facility that processes use to submit operations and suspend. Track pending operations in a map from op_id to (pid, callback_type). When a completion arrives, look up the waiting process, store the result, and wake it.

requirements.title: Pending operation registry
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL maintain a concurrent map from op_id (u64) to PendingIo { pid: u64, result_mode: ResultMode } that tracks which process is waiting for which I/O completion.
acceptance: PendingIoRegistry struct with DashMap<u64, PendingIo>
PendingIo struct: { pid: u64, result_mode: ResultMode }
ResultMode enum: XRegister (result in x(0) on resume), Message (result sent as message to mailbox), Discard (fire-and-forget)
register(op_id, pid, mode) and take(op_id) -> Option<PendingIo> methods
Thread-safe (DashMap)

requirements.title: Completion poller thread
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL spawn a completion poller thread that continuously calls ring.poll_completions() and dispatches each IoCompletion to the waiting process via the pending registry.
acceptance: IoCompletionBridge::start(ring: Arc<dyn CompletionRing>, registry: Arc<PendingIoRegistry>, scheduler: ...) spawns poller thread
Poller loop: poll_completions with 100ms timeout, process each completion
For each completion: take(op_id) from registry, look up process by pid
ResultMode::XRegister: store result term in process x(0), transition process from Waiting to Yielded, wake via condvar
ResultMode::Message: enqueue result as message in process mailbox, wake if process is in receive-wait
ResultMode::Discard: log completion, do not wake
Poller exits cleanly on bridge shutdown
Test: submit an op, register pending, simulate completion, verify process woken

requirements.title: IoFacility trait for process-side I/O submission
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define an IoFacility trait that processes use to submit I/O operations and suspend. THE SYSTEM SHALL inject the facility into NativeServices alongside existing facilities.
acceptance: IoFacility trait with submit_and_suspend(op: IoOp, mode: ResultMode) -> Result<(), IoError> method
Implementation: submit op to ring, register in pending registry with caller pid, return InstructionOutcome::Waiting to suspend the process
NativeServices gains io_facility: Option<Arc<dyn IoFacility>> field
ProcessContext gains io_facility() accessor
The IoFacility implementation holds references to the CompletionRing and PendingIoRegistry
Test: submit via facility, verify process transitions to Waiting

requirements.title: Scheduler integration — ring lifecycle and bridge startup
requirements.spec: WHEN the Scheduler is constructed, THE SYSTEM SHALL create the CompletionRing (via create_ring()) and start the IoCompletionBridge. THE SYSTEM SHALL inject the IoFacility into NativeServices for all worker threads.
acceptance: Scheduler::new creates the ring and bridge
Bridge poller thread starts alongside scheduler worker threads
IoFacility injected into NativeServices for all processes
Scheduler::shutdown() stops the bridge and ring cleanly
Ring and bridge are optional (None if IO is not configured) for test scenarios that don't need IO

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement specific I/O operations (file, TCP, UDP) — those are B-103 through B-111
SHALL NOT implement file descriptor lifecycle — that is B-102
SHALL NOT add new process status variants — use existing Waiting/Yielded transitions
SHALL NOT block scheduler worker threads — all I/O completion handling happens on the dedicated poller thread
verification: cargo test passes with bridge enabled and disabled (optional feature)
Process submits I/O op, suspends, and is woken when completion arrives
Multiple processes can have pending I/O concurrently without interference
Bridge shutdown is clean — no dangling threads, no stuck processes
Scheduler with no I/O configuration starts normally (bridge is None)
